{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 100,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing pipe guides adjacent to expansion loops allowing uncontrolled lateral thermal movement",
    "Expansion loops cannot function as designed without guides to direct axial movement and restrain lateral displacement",
    "In-service piping at risk of excessive lateral deflection, stress concentration at fittings, and accelerated hanger/support wear"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install pipe guides at proper locations near expansion loop anchors per project support standards and MSS SP-58",
    "Inspect adjacent hangers and supports for damage from uncontrolled movement and replace as needed",
    "Verify expansion loop design calculations and guide spacing to confirm correct placement",
    "Perform walkdown of all expansion loops on the system to identify any additional missing guides"
  ],
  "rationale": "Expansion loops require guides placed at specific offsets from the loop to direct thermal growth axially and prevent lateral buckling. Missing guides near expansion loops represent a non-compliant installation that defeats the purpose of the loop, allowing uncontrolled pipe movement under thermal cycling. In an in-service operating asset, this creates high risk of pipe displacement, support overload, and potential failure at connection points. MSS SP-58 and project support standards require proper guide placement adjacent to expansion loops; absence of these guides is a code non-compliance that warrants a fail decision.",
  "references": ["MSS SP-58", "project support standards"]
}
